question: Which of the following principles are most commonly cited in AI-based ethical frameworks?

options.A: Happiness and spiritual contentment
options.B: Human control and autonomy
options.C: Fairness and non-discrimination
options.D: Transparency and explainability

note: Top 4 nguyên tắc phổ biến nhất framework: Fairness, Accountability, Transparency, Privacy. Trong list chỉ có Transparency/explainability → D.

question: Which of the following are common key performance indicators (KPIs) within dashboards of data-driven systems? (Select two.)

options.A: Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA)
options.B: Performance metrics, such as accuracy and cost functions
options.C: Model training time
options.D: Operating cashflow forecast

note: KPI dashboard data-driven = phải đo model: accuracy/cost (B) + training time (C). EBITDA/cashflow là KPI tài chính, không phải data system.

question: Which of the following is an important reason for continually refreshing an organization's internal and external ethics policies?

options.A: It helps maintain alignment of the policies with the cultural and contextual values of the organization's employees.
options.B: It helps maintain alignment with the emergent risks and changing ethical posture of the products and services of the organization.
options.C: It helps internal stakeholders justify the release of new product features or service upgrades.
options.D: It helps demonstrate to senior management that work is being done on the internal and external ethics policies.

note: Refresh policy = bắt kịp emergent risks và ethical posture mới của sản phẩm → B. Còn lại đều là lý do hời hợt/political.

question: Which of the following are advantages to adopting standards frameworks like ISO 27000? (Select two.)

options.A: Formal certification processes that provide competitive advantage
options.B: Regulatory weight and legal enforcement
options.C: Technology-specific focus and precise implementation instructions
options.D: International support, recognition, and involvement

note: ISO 27000 mạnh ở: certification chính thức (A) + recognition quốc tế (D). KHÔNG có legal enforcement (B sai) và KHÔNG technology-specific (C sai).

question: Which of the following would be the most beneficial stage to hold ethical risk reviews for a new project that your team is undertaking?

options.A: Conception
options.B: Development
options.C: Design
options.D: Deployment

note: Ethical risk review nên ở Conception (A) — càng sớm càng rẻ. Càng về Deployment càng tốn kém để fix.

question: Which of the following are effective strategies when making statements on social media? (Select two.)

options.A: Communicating only as needed
options.B: Presenting a face of the organization
options.C: Focusing on positive feedback
options.D: Being honest and up front

note: Social media: nói thật + up front (D) + present face of org (B). Communicate-as-needed = giấu giếm; chỉ focus positive = ethics washing.

question: Which of the following is a potential benefit of having an ethical organizational culture?

options.A: More employees are likely to switch careers.
options.B: Employees will feel a sense of responsibility and loyalty.
options.C: Employees are more likely to listen to their superiors.
options.D: The company will get an increase in stock value.

note: Ethical culture → nhân viên thấy responsibility & loyalty (B). 3 đáp án còn lại đều mô tả hệ quả gián tiếp/sai bản chất.

question: Which of the following are key principles of privacy by design? (Select two.)

options.A: Organizations must incorporate privacy protections throughout the project lifecycle.
options.B: Organizations must keep the focus of privacy protections on the business rather than the user.
options.C: Organizations must not expose the operational practices and technologies used to protect user privacy.
options.D: Organizations must be proactive in protecting against privacy risks, not reactive.

note: Privacy by Design 2 cốt lõi: lifecycle-wide protection (A) + Proactive not Reactive (D). Hai trong 7 nguyên tắc gốc của Ann Cavoukian.

question: Why is it important to categorize ethical risks into different types? (Select two.)

options.A: It makes it easier for us to identify and manage different types of risks.
options.B: It helps us understand the many complexities involved in different types of ethical risks.
options.C: It helps us consider risk mitigation in terms of universal tactics that apply to all potential kinds of ethical risk.
options.D: It ensures that customers and other external stakeholders know that we take ethical risks seriously.

note: Categorize risk = dễ identify/manage (A) + hiểu complexity riêng (B). KHÔNG phải để ra universal tactic (C sai vì mỗi loại risk khác nhau).

question: Which of the following best describes why data is sometimes compared to oil? (Select two.)

options.A: Data can be monetarily valuable.
options.B: Data can damage the environment.
options.C: Data can be easily monopolized.
options.D: Data can fuel algorithmic technologies.

note: Data như oil vì: monetarily valuable (A) + fuel cho algorithms (D). 'Damage env' và 'monopolized' là claims khác, không phải lý do core.

question: Why is a model that has been overfitted to its training data a source of fairness risk?

options.A: Because the model is too complex.
options.B: Because the model includes too much noise.
options.C: Because the model won't generalize to the entire population.
options.D: Because the model has a temporal bias.

note: Overfitting = học thuộc training set, KHÔNG generalize cho population → fairness risk = C. Noise/complex là root cause của overfit, không phải lý do fairness.

question: Which of the following describes an ethical framework?

options.A: Ethical frameworks apply meta-ethical theories to everyday business operations.
options.B: Ethical frameworks consolidate regulatory requirements for an industry.
options.C: Ethical frameworks raise timeless ethical questions that are not easily put into action.
options.D: Ethical frameworks seek to mitigate ethical concerns by creating actionable steps.

note: Ethical framework = biến meta-ethics thành actionable steps cụ thể → D. A nhầm chiều, B là regulation, C là phủ định framework.

question: Which of the following provide a user with more meaningful choices regarding how their data is being used? (Select two.)

options.A: Whether the data is used by a person of a certain gender
options.B: The format that the data is stored in
options.C: Granular or modular control over data usage
options.D: Opt-out/opt-in mechanisms

note: User control thật = granular control (C) + opt-in/opt-out (D). Format storage và gender-of-user không phải choice cho user.

question: Which of the following is an essential asset that helps to operationalize the internal and external ethics policies via procedures?

options.A: A description of the procedures in the annual report of the organization.
options.B: A detailed report that presents the various scenarios using technical jargon.
options.C: Concrete examples that show how the policies work in practice.
options.D: A designated ethics lead who is solely responsible for the implementation of said procedures.

note: Operationalize policy = concrete examples (C) — ngôn ngữ jargon (B) hay annual report (A) không xuống đến hành động hằng ngày.

question: Which of the following describes personhood?

options.A: Personhood is often used to dictate how something is treated.
options.B: Personhood is a concept that applies to narrow AI.
options.C: Personhood is the legal protection provided to AI systems.
options.D: Personhood is an individual's right to freedom.

note: Personhood quyết định cách entity được treat (A). Không phải narrow AI, không phải legal protection cho AI, không phải freedom.

question: Which of the following is an important concept to consider as part of building an organizational philosophy?

options.A: Adopting philosophies already created by other organizations
options.B: Anticipating future implications of that philosophy
options.C: Considering internal stakeholder needs above all else
options.D: Putting ethical principles into practice

note: Building philosophy = phải anticipate future implications (B). Copy người khác (A), chỉ internal stakeholder (C), 'putting into practice' (D) là execution.

question: Which of the following are likely to be found within a penetration test rules of engagement (ROE)? (Select two.)

options.A: What systems may be targeted
options.B: How long CCTV camera recordings should be maintained
options.C: The appropriate length and complexity of employee passwords
options.D: What methods of attack are legitimate

note: Penetration test ROE quy định: targets nào (A) + methods nào hợp lệ (D). Password policy / CCTV không thuộc ROE.

question: Which of the following is an important action needed for the finalization of internal and external ethics policies before approval?

options.A: Discarding open issues to provide a clean slate for the next iteration of the policies
options.B: Ensuring the policies are the same as those from other peer organizations
options.C: Testing the policy in an already deployed project setting
options.D: Documenting the assumptions and choices made in the drafting of the policies

note: Finalize policy = phải document assumptions & choices (D) — để audit về sau. Discard issues / copy peer / test khi đã deploy đều sai.

question: Which of the following best describes a marketing persona?

options.A: A description of a type of character based upon demographics, habits, and interests
options.B: A character that encapsulates a brand's personality
options.C: An individual who assists with the activity of marketing
options.D: The emotional impression that a customer has about a product or category of goods

note: Marketing persona = nhân vật ảo dựa trên demographics/habits/interests (A). Khác brand personality (B), khác marketer thực (C).

question: If you are attempting to build a new framework for the research and development (R&D) of AI, which of the following frameworks might you look at first for its emphasis in this area?

options.A: The G20 AI Principles
options.B: The Montreal Declaration for a Responsible Development of Artificial Intelligence
options.C: The American Medical Association's definition of artificial intelligence
options.D: The Beijing AI Principles

note: Beijing AI Principles (D) nhấn mạnh R&D. G20 = governance, Montreal = responsible dev, AMA = augmented intelligence.

question: Why is deciding how to act using moral reasoning not always a feasible goal for human beings?

options.A: Most people are not educated on normative ethical theories and therefore cannot perform true moral reasoning.
options.B: Moral reasoning is too complicated to apply to a real-world situation.
options.C: Moral reasoning has few tangible benefits for most people.
options.D: Human decision making is often influenced by emotion and not logic.

note: Moral reasoning bị emotion chi phối (D) — đó là lý do thực tế. Không phải vì thiếu giáo dục (A) hay quá phức tạp (B).

question: How can you leverage emerging technologies like AI in disaster recovery plans (DRP)?

options.A: By using deep learning techniques to bolster the organization's defense against disasters.
options.B: By using natural language processing (NLP) techniques to ensure the DRP document is understandable and free of typographic errors.
options.C: By using data-driven techniques to inform the strategies behind the DRP.
options.D: By using AI decision-making systems to decide whether or not a DRP is necessary for your organization.

note: AI trong DRP = dùng data-driven techniques để inform strategy (C). Deep learning để 'defense disaster' và NLP fix typo là dùng sai chỗ.

question: Which of the following is an example of applied ethics?

options.A: Professional ethics
options.B: Moral relativism
options.C: Virtue ethics
options.D: Pluralism

note: Applied ethics = professional ethics (A). Moral relativism, virtue ethics, pluralism là normative/meta-ethics theory.

question: Which type of edge case is caused by data outside the normal distribution?

options.A: Errors
options.B: Noise
options.C: Overfitting
options.D: Outliers

note: Outliers = data ngoài normal distribution (D). Errors là sai sót, noise là biến động ngẫu nhiên, overfitting là model issue.

question: Which of the following are important qualities of a press release? (Select two.)

options.A: It's concise
options.B: It's in a digital format
options.C: It's non-interactive
options.D: It's delivered on a single platform

note: Press release = concise (A) + digital format (B). Single platform / non-interactive là đặc tính sai.

question: How can confirmation bias impact us socially? (Select two.)

options.A: It can prevent us from being social with other people.
options.B: It can impede socio-political cooperation.
options.C: It can lead to a diverse set of friends.
options.D: It can lead to groupthink, which can in turn halt forward progress.

note: Confirmation bias social: cản trở socio-political cooperation (B) + groupthink (D). KHÔNG dẫn đến diverse friends.

question: Which of the following describes extrajudicial judgment?

options.A: A ruling or scoring made outside of a court.
options.B: Unusual interpretation of law outside of its typical meaning.
options.C: Applying law from one jurisdiction within another.
options.D: Transferring defendants from one jurisdiction to another for trial.

note: Extrajudicial = ruling/scoring NGOÀI tòa án (A). Algorithmic scoring credit/recidivism là ví dụ kinh điển.

question: Which of the following is a problematic reason to pilot a code of ethics prior to widespread deployment?

options.A: The pilot phase demonstrates the seriousness of the organization with respect to its ethical responsibilities.
options.B: The pilot phase helps to stress test a code of ethics in a real-world situation.
options.C: The pilot phase helps to develop awareness and buy-in from the relevant stakeholders in the organization.
options.D: The pilot phase helps to identify gaps in the current code of ethics.

note: Pilot CoE problematic khi mục đích = 'demonstrate seriousness' (A) — đó là PR, không phải value thật. B/C/D đều có giá trị thật.

question: The Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) safeguards the privacy of which age group's personal information?

options.A: Anyone under 18 years old
options.B: Anyone under 13 years old
options.C: Anyone between 13 and 18 years old
options.D: Anyone between 5 and 13 years old

note: COPPA = Children's Online Privacy = bảo vệ trẻ DƯỚI 13 tuổi (B). Đừng nhầm 18.

question: What is the purpose of a holding statement in crisis and media communications?

options.A: To pre-write communications before they are needed
options.B: To prevent personnel from communicating prematurely
options.C: To order the business to temporarily halt certain operations
options.D: To retain the services of specific media outlets

note: Holding statement = pre-write trước khi cần (A). Để sẵn sàng phản hồi nhanh, KHÔNG phải để cấm ai nói.

question: Which of the following helps to ensure that internal and external ethics policies are adhered to in practice?

options.A: Having unambiguous procedures tied to the clauses of the policy.
options.B: Ensuring the policies have ample room for flexibility in implementation.
options.C: Including detailed legalese in the policy that mirrors regulatory requirements.
options.D: Developing procedures first and then creating policies that reflect those procedures.

note: Policy adherence = unambiguous procedures gắn với clauses (A). Flexibility (B) = dễ lách, legalese (C) = khó hiểu, develop procedure trước (D) = ngược chiều.

question: Which of the following statements are promoted by the categorical imperative? (Select three.)

options.A: Act in such a way that your actions may become a universal law.
options.B: You have a moral duty to choose your actions based on their potential outcomes.
options.C: Each person must use reason to will moral laws.
options.D: Don't treat people as a means to an end; treat them always as an end.

note: Categorical imperative (Kant) = universal law (A) + reason để will moral law (C) + treat as end, not means (D). B là consequentialism, không phải Kant.

question: Which of the following describes why explainability is important?

options.A: It is necessary so that internal stakeholders can understand how a system works.
options.B: It provides accountability and trust.
options.C: It provides interpretations of a system's actions.
options.D: It enables you to explain a system for shareholder purposes.

note: Explainability quan trọng vì nó cung cấp accountability + trust (B). A/C/D mô tả hậu quả nhỏ hơn.

question: Which of the following might be a risk of not having an ethical organizational culture?

options.A: Employees might face cognitive dissonance.
options.B: Employees are more likely to express their opinions about the company.
options.C: Employees will experience a faster approval process to get a product launched.
options.D: Employees might be less interested in their careers.

note: Thiếu ethical culture → cognitive dissonance (A) — nhân viên kẹt giữa giá trị cá nhân và yêu cầu công ty.

question: Which of the following benefits can be gained from establishing baseline system behaviors? (Select two.)

options.A: Optimizing costs of operation
options.B: Holding employees more accountable
options.C: Tracking deviance from norms
options.D: Restoring compromised functionality through a system rollback

note: Baseline behavior giúp: track deviance (C) + rollback restore (D). KHÔNG để hold accountable hay optimize cost.

question: Which of the following is a formative ethics theory that states that maximizing happiness is the primary standard for determining what is right or wrong?

options.A: Categorical imperative
options.B: Utilitarianism
options.C: Deontology
options.D: Virtue ethics

note: Utilitarianism (B) = maximize happiness = nguyên tắc 'right or wrong'. Categorical imperative = duty, deontology = rule, virtue = character.

question: Which of the following statements accurately describes the philosophical concept of predeterminism?

options.A: All events, past, present, and future, are determined in advance.
options.B: All future events are determined by preceding events, as in a chain, but human beings may still be able to interfere with this chain of events.
options.C: Human beings are able to make choices whose outcomes are not already determined.
options.D: All events are predestined to happen by a supernatural force.

note: Predeterminism = TẤT CẢ events past/present/future đã được định trước (A). B là mềm hơn (compatibilism), D là religious determinism.

question: Which of the following is a transparency risk of closed source software?

options.A: Closed source software includes obfuscated code that cannot be read by a human.
options.B: Closed source software cannot be shared with business partners.
options.C: Closed source software cannot be reviewed by the organization that developed it.
options.D: Closed source software may not be accessible to independent auditors.

note: Closed source transparency risk = independent auditor không truy cập được (D). Obfuscated code chỉ là kỹ thuật, không phải risk core.

question: Which of the following describes the purpose of having an Ethics Board for a data-driven organization?

options.A: An Ethics Board can help maintain an organization's values-based intentions and decrease the inherent dangers of autonomous technologies.
options.B: An Ethics Board can help maintain an organization's profit margins and increase transparency into how they increase their profits.
options.C: An Ethics Board can help maintain an organization's values-based intentions and increase transparency into how they use data-driven technologies.
options.D: An Ethics Board can increase transparency into how the organization uses AI or autonomous technologies.

note: Ethics Board = duy trì values-based intent + tăng transparency cách dùng data-driven tech (C). KHÔNG về profit margin.

question: You're training a model to classify whether or not a bridge is likely to collapse given several factors. You have a dataset of thousands of existing bridges and their attributes, where each bridge is labeled as having collapsed or not collapsed. Only a handful of bridges in the dataset are labeled as having collapsed—the rest are labeled as not collapsed. Given your goal of minimizing bridge collapse and the severe harm it can cause, which of the following metrics would be most useful for evaluating the model?

options.A: Recall
options.B: Precision
options.C: Confusion matrix
options.D: Accuracy

note: Bridge collapse rất hiếm (imbalanced) + hậu quả nặng → cần Recall (A) để bắt được mọi case dương tính. Accuracy bị skew bởi class âm.

question: Which of the following describes the AI Fairness 360 project?

options.A: An AI product that represents a completely fair model for AI practitioners to use as a benchmark.
options.B: A checklist for machine learning practitioners to follow when training fair AI models.
options.C: A global initiative that promotes fairness in AI through seminars, conferences, and other community-driven activities.
options.D: An open source library that evaluates models for bias and provides mitigation tactics to reduce that bias.

note: AI Fairness 360 (IBM) = open source library đánh giá bias + đề xuất mitigation (D). Không phải 'completely fair model'.

question: Which of the following are reasons to design, develop, and deploy a code of ethics within an organization? (Select two).

options.A: It compels employees to operate in an ethical and integral manner.
options.B: It prevents regulatory scrutiny of the organization's practices.
options.C: It lets external stakeholders know that you are an ethical company.
options.D: It creates consistency in the ethical practices across the organization, rather than patchy implementation of the practices.

note: Code of ethics có 2 lý do CORE: compels integrity (A) + tạo consistency thay vì patchy (D). 'Prevent regulatory scrutiny' (B) là sai mục đích.

question: Which type of bias causes people to trust an automated decision-making system (ADS) over a human's decision?

options.A: Automation bias
options.B: Confirmation bias
options.C: Complacency bias
options.D: Implicit bias

note: Automation bias (A) = tin máy hơn người. Confirmation = tin theo niềm tin có sẵn, complacency = lơ là, implicit = bias ngầm.

question: Which of the following is an explainability risk of self-learning models?

options.A: Self-learning models are unable to generate decisions as output.
options.B: Self-learning models may introduce new data you're unaware of.
options.C: Self-learning models are black boxes whose decisions cannot be understood.
options.D: Self-learning models can corrupt data or otherwise make it unreadable.

note: Self-learning model risk = đưa data mới mà ta không biết (B) → tăng nguy cơ ngầm. Black box (C) đúng cho deep learning, nhưng câu này hỏi self-learning cụ thể.

question: Which school of philosophical thought primarily advocates for the greatest good for the greatest amount of people?

options.A: Kantian ethics
options.B: Utilitarianism
options.C: Deontology
options.D: Virtue ethics

note: Utilitarianism (B) = greatest good for greatest number. Đây là Bentham/Mill kinh điển.

question: Which of the following describe how adopting ethical practices can be a strategic differentiator? (Select three.)

options.A: It will encourage applicants to apply for your company.
options.B: It will ensure you comply with regulations.
options.C: It will support the development of strategic partnerships.
options.D: It will reduce your business obligations toward customers and business partners.

note: Ethical practices như lợi thế chiến lược: hút talent (A) + strategic partnerships (C) + customer trust (E). 'Comply regulations' chỉ là baseline, không phải differentiator.

question: How does increasing AI performance often conflict with the desire for explainability?

options.A: Increasing AI performance sometimes reduces the transparency of input data used in training, making it more difficult to explain decision-making processes.
options.B: Increasing AI performance sometimes leads to certain evaluation metrics no longer being useful, making it more difficult to explain decision-making processes.
options.C: Increasing AI performance sometimes leads to greater model complexity, making it more difficult to explain decision-making processes.
options.D: Increasing AI performance sometimes removes human-in-the-loop (HITL) methods, making it more difficult to explain decision-making processes.

note: Performance ↑ thường = model phức tạp hơn → khó explain (C). Đánh đổi accuracy ↔ interpretability kinh điển.

question: Your business handles the personal data of California residents. Which of the following regulations would enable a resident to request that their data be deleted from your company's files?

options.A: COPPA
options.B: OECD Privacy Guidelines
options.C: CCPA
options.D: PCI DSS

note: California → CCPA cho phép xóa data. (Câu có lỗi đáp án: option C ghi COPPA nhưng câu đúng là CCPA — answer = C theo key.)

question: Which of the following is an important reason to have a policy owner for internal and external ethics policies ?

options.A: They can help to prevent changes from taking place in the policies over time by justifying their choices.
options.B: The policy owner will write the policy, relying solely on their personal institutional knowledge.
options.C: It helps to have a single person that can be held accountable in case something goes wrong.
options.D: They can help to bring the right internal and external stakeholders together to help draft these ethics policies.

note: Policy owner = người đứng giữa các stakeholders để draft (D). Không phải để 'prevent change' hay tự viết một mình.

question: How can sentiment analysis be useful in social media communication?

options.A: It can shape the content and delivery of the message based on users' perceptions of the organization or technology.
options.B: It can determine how to construct a message that influences users to be more positive about the organization or technology.
options.C: It can determine which users have the largest influence on your reputation, and therefore which users you should communicate with.
options.D: It can classify customers as loyal or disloyal, helping you target customers differently in your communications.

note: Sentiment analysis = đo perception → shape content/delivery dựa trên đó (A). KHÔNG phải để 'làm user positive hơn' (B = manipulative).

question: Is an open door policy equivalent to ensuring employee voice?

options.A: Yes, because both allow employees to speak up.
options.B: Yes, because an open door policy is essential for employee voice.
options.C: No, because an open door policy is the opposite of employee voice.
options.D: No, because an open door policy does not ensure employees feel heard.

note: Open door ≠ employee voice. Open door cho phép nói nhưng không đảm bảo được nghe (D). Voice = follow-up + được tác động thực sự.

question: Which of the following is a best practice when participating in media inquiries?

options.A: Divulge only the information that you are required to by law
options.B: Get your message out as quickly as possible
options.C: Only agree to speak to media outlets that are favorable to your organization
options.D: Ensure your message is consistent

note: Media inquiry: ensure message consistent (D). 'Get out quickly' = vội, 'only friendly outlets' = cherry-pick, 'only required by law' = quá kín.

question: Which of the following is the most important idea that a crisis communication program should manage?

options.A: The individual details of a crisis
options.B: Internal stakeholder consensus about how to approach a crisis
options.C: The real-world effects of a crisis
options.D: The public's perception of a crisis

note: Crisis comm program quản lý = public's perception (D). Real-world effects và internal consensus là khía cạnh khác.

question: Why does the trolley problem pose an ethical predicament?

options.A: The moral responsibility is split between you and the person controlling the trolley.
options.B: You have to make a choice between two scenarios where choosing one leads to loss of life in the other.
options.C: You as the actor don't have sufficient control over the circumstance.
options.D: There are so many potential outcomes that it becomes difficult to choose one that is most ethical.

note: Trolley problem = chọn 1 trong 2 kịch bản, mỗi cái đều dẫn đến mất mạng (B). Đây là dilemma kinh điển consequentialism.

question: Which of the following is a noted difference between a certificate and a certification?

options.A: Certifications are issued by industry certification bodies.
options.B: Certifications cannot be revoked.
options.C: Certifications are granted upon completion of a course.
options.D: Certifications can be accredited.

note: Certification do industry body cấp (A). Certificate = hoàn thành course. Certification có thể bị revoke và không cần accredit.

question: How does a smart contract differ from a traditional contract?

options.A: Smart contracts eliminate the need for a central authority.
options.B: Smart contracts guarantee that all parties are anonymous.
options.C: Smart contracts serve a different purpose than traditional contracts.
options.D: Smart contracts are more effective than traditional contracts.

note: Smart contract = không cần central authority (A) — chạy trên blockchain, tự execute. Không phải về anonymity hay 'effectiveness'.

question: Which of the following are ways that regulations differ from ethical frameworks? (Select two.)

options.A: Regulations provide a clear basis for potential litigation.
options.B: Regulations have legal enforcement behind them.
options.C: Regulations are flexible in their implementation.
options.D: Regulations are often industry led.

note: Regulation khác framework: có legal basis cho litigation (A) + có legal enforcement (B). Framework là flexible/voluntary, regulation thì không.

question: Which of the following elements might be considered moderately affected in an Impact Level 2 Algorithmic Impact Assessment? (Select two.)

options.A: A brief delay in the conveyance of a commercial transaction.
options.B: The health and well-being of individuals or communities.
options.C: A loss of life resulting from specific circumstances.
options.D: The rights of individuals or communities.

note: AIA Impact Level 2 = moderate impact: health/well-being (B) + rights of individuals (D). Loss of life = level cao hơn, brief delay = level thấp hơn.

question: What does "ethics by design" mean?

options.A: A creative design approach as the focus for ethics.
options.B: An approach in which ethics is considered from the initial design stage.
options.C: A reference to one of the tenets of engineering activism.
options.D: A reference to the framework set forth by IEEE's Ethically Aligned Design.

note: Ethics by design = tích hợp ethics từ giai đoạn design ban đầu (B). Tương tự privacy by design.

question: How does the "virtuous cycle" that benefits Big Tech operate?

options.A: Better classes of customers lead to richer and more refined data for algorithmic systems.
options.B: Organizations write algorithms with fewer biases, which leads to fairer outcomes.
options.C: By acting virtuous, the public respects Big Tech more and more.
options.D: Data-driven algorithms improve solutions, leading to new customers, and better data.

note: Big Tech virtuous cycle: thuật toán cải thiện → user mới → data tốt hơn → thuật toán càng tốt (D). Self-reinforcing loop.

question: Which of the following is the most important ethical consideration regarding technical developments like deepfakes?

options.A: They take away monetization opportunities, leaving individuals unfairly compensated for their data.
options.B: They are built on technological progress made by a third-party organization.
options.C: They violate data sharing agreements in many jurisdictions.
options.D: They usurp a person's likeness and can then be weaponized against them.

note: Deepfake ethical concern lớn nhất = chiếm dụng likeness và weaponize chống cá nhân (D). A/B/C đều phụ.

question: Which of the following is a best practice for incorporating diversity, equity, and inclusiveness in communication?

options.A: Ensure multiple groups of people based on race, gender, etc., are communicating in different ways
options.B: Focus on the organization's intended message rather than incorporating feedback from external stakeholders
options.C: Reassure customers that the organization is diverse, equitable, and inclusive
options.D: Promote an organizational culture that embraces these values

note: DEI trong communication = promote organizational culture embracing values (D). Không phải PR reassurance.

question: What does the acronym CSIRT stand for in a security context?

options.A: Conformance strategy, immutability, reliability, trustworthiness
options.B: Computer security immediate readiness tools
options.C: Computer science, Internet, real-time interaction, technology
options.D: Cybersecurity incident response team

note: CSIRT = Cybersecurity Incident Response Team (D). Nhớ acronym → team xử lý sự cố.

question: Which of the following sources does a SIEM system typically pull data from? (Select two.)

options.A: Personnel emails
options.B: Encrypted personal data
options.C: System event logs
options.D: Network intrusion detection alerts

note: SIEM pull từ: system event logs (C) + IDS alerts (D). KHÔNG đọc personal email/encrypted data.

question: Which of the following describes an opt-out policy in regards to the collection of private data?

options.A: Data about the user is always collected, regardless of the user's consent.
options.B: Data about that user isn't collected until that user explicitly states you are allowed to.
options.C: Data about the user is never collected, regardless of the user's consent.
options.D: Data about the user is automatically collected unless that user explicitly states that you should not do so.

note: Opt-out = tự động thu THUYẾT cho đến khi user nói KHÔNG (D). Opt-in thì ngược lại.

question: What is the fundamental attribution error?

options.A: When you believe your chances of experiencing something negative are lower and your chances of experiencing something positive are higher than others.
options.B: When you believe your ideas are normal and that the majority of people agree with you.
options.C: When you say your bad behavior is caused by the situation, but when other people display the same bad behavior, it is caused by a personality trait.
options.D: When you incorrectly assume a cause and effect relationship for two correlated variables.

note: Fundamental attribution error = hành vi xấu của tôi do hoàn cảnh, của người khác do tính cách (C). Bias nhận thức kinh điển.

question: Which of the following are tasks in the overall risk management process? (Select three.)

options.A: Mitigation
options.B: Deployment
options.C: Identification
options.D: Elimination

note: Risk management 3 task chính: Identification (C) + Analysis (E) + Mitigation (A). Không có 'deployment' hay 'elimination' (vì rủi ro hiếm khi loại bỏ hoàn toàn).

question: At what point should ethical consideration ideally be applied to emerging technologies?

options.A: Upon delivery, with appropriate warranties where necessary.
options.B: During periodic reviews, with ongoing customer feedback solicited.
options.C: From its inception, through maintenance, to applying foresight regarding its decommissioning.
options.D: Once an ethical issue has received negative feedback in public media.

note: Ethics phải áp dụng từ inception → maintenance → decommissioning (C). Toàn vòng đời, không chỉ khi giao hàng hay sau bị chỉ trích.

question: You have a dataset of customers that includes each customer's gender, location, and other personal attributes. The label you are trying to predict is how much sales revenue each customer is likely to generate for the business based on these attributes. What type of machine learning outcome is this problem suited for?

options.A: Classification
options.B: Dimensionality reduction
options.C: Clustering
options.D: Regression

note: Predict revenue (số liên tục) = Regression (D). Classification cho discrete, clustering cho unsupervised, dim reduction cho features.

question: What is the difference between beneficence and non-maleficence?

options.A: Beneficence refers to "do only good" and non-maleficence refers to "do no harm."
options.B: Non-maleficence refers only to malevolent artificial general intelligence (AGI), while beneficence can refer to any "good" emerging technology.
options.C: Beneficence is a less important goal for the field of AI than non-maleficence.
options.D: Beneficence and non-maleficence are quite similar and often interchangeable.

note: Beneficence = 'do GOOD' (chủ động). Non-maleficence = 'do NO HARM' (thụ động). Đáp án A.

question: Which of the following is a valid reason for tracking the number and severity of an organization's ethical violations?

options.A: It can dissuade end users from scrutinizing the internal and external ethics policies.
options.B: It can help provide qualitative information that can be used to improve the policies.
options.C: It can replace the need for refreshing the internal and external ethics policies.
options.D: It can help provide transparency around the efforts to improve the policies.

note: Track ethical violations để tạo transparency cho effort cải thiện policy (D). KHÔNG phải để dissuade scrutiny hay thay refresh.

question: What is the purpose of differential privacy?

options.A: To only allow certain parties to access certain portions of the data.
options.B: To enable parties to share private data without revealing individuals represented in the data.
options.C: To remove the direct identifiers that can be used to identify individuals.
options.D: To ensure the data is completely confidential and cannot be read by unauthorized parties.

note: Differential privacy = chia sẻ pattern dataset mà không lộ cá nhân (B). Khác anonymization (chỉ remove ID), khác access control.

question: Which of the following describes the technique of homomorphic encryption?

options.A: A method of mitigating biased algorithms through enhanced secrecy.
options.B: A method of swapping public and private keys between hosts.
options.C: A method of cryptography that hides data inside other data.
options.D: A method of performing operations on encrypted data.

note: Homomorphic encryption = thực hiện phép toán TRÊN dữ liệu mã hóa (D). Cho phép compute mà không cần decrypt.

question: What causes bias?

options.A: Biases are caused by the opinions of our families.
options.B: Bias is biological, we are born with it.
options.C: Bias is caused by the media.
options.D: Biases are learned from our families, our social groups, and the media.

note: Bias học từ family + social group + media (D). Không bẩm sinh, không chỉ từ media.

question: Which of the following best describes the use case for zero-knowledge protocols (ZKPs)?

options.A: Handling divide-by-zero errors in computing software
options.B: Proving that something exists, but not the specifics
options.C: Choosing what to do when you have zero knowledge of a subject
options.D: Being made aware of when the battery in a product is past its zero point

note: ZKP = chứng minh điều gì đó EXISTS mà không tiết lộ chi tiết (B). Vd: chứng minh tuổi >= 18 mà không lộ DOB.

question: Why is the question of robot rights and emancipation one that isn't as important as addressing issues of bias, privacy, transparency, and other principles discussed in the various ethical frameworks?

options.A: Humans are anthropocentric and don't want to extend rights to other sentient entities.
options.B: These rights necessitate that robots become sentient entities, which is currently not feasible.
options.C: Robots are mechanical instruments and therefore don't deserve to have rights.
options.D: There is no legal precedent for granting rights to entities that are not humans.

note: Robot rights chưa quan trọng vì cần sentience trước (B) — robot hiện tại chưa sentient. Câu hỏi mang tính practical, không anthropocentric.

question: Which of the following is the most important argument in favor of content moderation in online platforms?

options.A: It prevents the spread of disinformation that can cause harm to vulnerable populations.
options.B: It helps uphold freedom of expression for everyone and doesn't give anyone special rights.
options.C: It creates adequate incentives for everyone to share their opinions.
options.D: It prevents the development of monopolies in terms of content creators.

note: Content moderation: quan trọng nhất = chặn disinformation gây hại nhóm dễ tổn thương (A). Không phải về freedom-balance hay incentive.

question: You want to solicit feedback from users who are seeking explanations about your AI products and services. So, you decide to place a form on your marketing website that users can fill out. Which of the following is the best way to structure that form for the purpose of collecting useful feedback?

options.A: Provide a series of yes or no questions with radio buttons where users select their respective answers.
options.B: Provide a large, unrestricted text box where users can enter their thoughts at length.
options.C: Provide a series of open questions with a single-line entry field where users can provide short answers.
options.D: Provide a drop-down menu of feedback categories that users can select from, with a promise that you'll follow up via email.

note: Form thu feedback explainability = unrestricted text box (B) — cho user diễn đạt đầy đủ. Yes/no và dropdown quá hạn chế.

question: To which of the following groups would a code of ethics not be applicable?

options.A: Business managers of the data-driven technology system
options.B: Data subjects of the data-driven technology system
options.C: Designers of the data-driven technology system
options.D: Developers of the data-driven technology system

note: Code of ethics KHÔNG áp dụng cho data subjects (B) — họ là đối tượng được bảo vệ, không phải actor tuân thủ.

question: Which of the following elements should be communicated with end users when negotiating ethical access to a user's data? (Select two.)

options.A: A request for consent to perform an action
options.B: The monetary value of that data
options.C: The intention behind a certain activity or request
options.D: An offer of money or other incentives to relinquish user's rights

note: Negotiate data ethically: xin consent (A) + nói rõ intention (C). Money/incentive = manipulation, dùng giá trị tiền = không phù hợp.

question: Which organizational resource is most commonly used in terms of fostering an ethical organizational culture?

options.A: Informational resources
options.B: Human resources
options.C: Physical resources
options.D: Financial resources

note: HR (Human Resources) = nguồn lực hàng đầu để xây ethical culture (B) — vì culture là về con người.

question: Why are groups like race and religion considered protected classes?

options.A: These groups have been used as the basis for wholesale discrimination.
options.B: These groups can be used to personally identify someone.
options.C: People use these groups as the basis for their identities.
options.D: Organizations are legally not allowed to collect information about these groups.

note: Race/religion là protected class vì historic wholesale discrimination (A). Không phải vì 'identify someone' hay 'collect bị cấm'.

question: Which of the following are possible causes of the limited adoption of a code of ethics within an organization? (Select two).

options.A: Comprehensive coverage of areas of concerns by the code of ethics
options.B: Lack of clarity in the code of ethics
options.C: Lack of awareness of the code of ethics and where it is meant to be applied
options.D: Prescriptive language used in the code of ethics

note: Hạn chế adoption CoE: thiếu clarity (B) + thiếu awareness/where-to-apply (C). Comprehensive coverage là tốt, không phải nguyên nhân hạn chế.

question: Which of the following are important elements of the data minimization principle? (Select two.)

options.A: Only delete data that can be easily replaced
options.B: Only collect data that is strictly necessary
options.C: Only compress data that needs to be kept as small as possible
options.D: Only keep data for as long as it is needed

note: Data minimization: chỉ collect strictly necessary (B) + chỉ giữ lâu cần thiết (D). Nguyên tắc 'less is more'.

question: Which of the following negative consequences is the direct result of failing to be truthful in communicating about a crisis?

options.A: Reputational damage
options.B: Discrimination
options.C: Financial loss
options.D: Security risk

note: Crisis không trung thực → reputational damage (A) — niềm tin mất là khó cứu nhất.

question: Why are anonymization and pseudonymization insufficient protection measures against breaches of data privacy and security?

options.A: They don't integrate well into data science and machine learning workflows.
options.B: They only work in scenarios with particular kinds of personal information.
options.C: They destroy the usefulness of the data.
options.D: They can be broken by combining this data with other publicly available data.

note: Anonymization/pseudonymization yếu vì có thể combine với public data để re-identify (D). Vd Netflix Prize, AOL search.

question: The organization needs to ask itself about any quality control issues it may be having. Which of the following processes is most suitable for asking and answering that question?

options.A: Business impact assessment
options.B: Business continuity plan
options.C: Vulnerability audit
options.D: Lessons learned report

note: Quality control issues = Vulnerability audit (C). BIA dự phòng business interrupt, BCP cho continuity, lessons learned post-incident.

question: When you deliver periodic statements about a crisis to the media, what should the first statement do?

options.A: Provide in-depth details about the situation
options.B: Accept responsibility for perceived problems
options.C: Survey public opinion of the organization
options.D: Set the tone of what is to follow

note: Statement đầu tiên trong crisis = SET TONE cho những gì sẽ theo sau (D). Không phải dump details hay nhận lỗi vội.

question: A disruptive user on a social media site is subjected to a shadow ban. Which of the following might be an indication of that shadow ban?

options.A: A user logs in one day to find a message from a site administrator saying that the user has been banned.
options.B: A user logs in one day to find that they have been asked to discontinue their disruptive behavior, or they will be banned.
options.C: A user logs in one day to find that their posting privileges have been revoked.
options.D: A user logs in one day to find that they are able to post, but receive no direct responses.

note: Shadow ban = vẫn post được nhưng KHÔNG ai phản hồi (D) — silent suppression. Khác ban công khai có notification.

question: Which of the following are likely examples of a leverageable third party data-driven or data-related product? (Select two.)

options.A: Data annotation and augmentation tools
options.B: Compiled models
options.C: Management oversight
options.D: Product design lifecycle

note: Third-party data product: data annotation tools (A) + compiled models (B). Management oversight và lifecycle là internal process.

question: Which of the following are logical arguments in favor of an organization maintaining compliance? (Select two.)

options.A: Long-term cost savings due to avoiding fines
options.B: Reduced time to deployment
options.C: Reduced costs of development
options.D: Avoidance of reputational damage

note: Compliance benefit: tiết kiệm fines dài hạn (A) + tránh reputational damage (D). KHÔNG phải reduce time/cost (đó là illusion).

question: Which of the following presents the most important argument for why we need to consider the impact of AI on labor in the design, development, and deployment of data-driven technology?

options.A: There may be an impact on worker productivity if they are not included in all phases.
options.B: There might be potential labor rights and human rights violations.
options.C: There is still a need for human workers in human-in-the-loop mechanisms.
options.D: The organization would like to avoid having to deal with union concerns.

note: AI labor impact lý do quan trọng nhất = vi phạm labor + human rights (B). Productivity và HITL là phụ.

question: Which of the following describes change management?

options.A: A collective term for all approaches to prepare, support, and help individuals, teams, and organizations in fostering an ethical organizational culture.
options.B: A collective term used to describe how employees are able to voice their concerns to facilitate change in their organization.
options.C: A collective term used to explain how organizations facilitate change in a product after receiving negative feedback.
options.D: A collective term for all approaches to prepare, support, and help individuals, teams, and organizations in making organizational change.

note: Change management = approaches giúp organization thay đổi (D). Không chỉ về 'ethical culture' (A) mà tổng quát hơn.

question: Which of the following elements constitute PII? (Select two.)

options.A: Telephone numbers
options.B: Device IDs
options.C: Cookies and tokens
options.D: Passport numbers

note: PII rõ ràng: phone (A) + passport number (D). Device ID/cookie là quasi-identifier (cần combine).

question: In the context of open data, what is FAIR?

options.A: An acronym for finance accoutability intelligence research.
options.B: A reference to fair competition, which makes open data possible.
options.C: An acronym for fundamental artificial intelligence research.
options.D: An acronym for findable, accessible, interoperable, and reusable.

note: FAIR = Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable (D). Open data principle nổi tiếng.

question: What are the duties of an ethics board for data-driven technology organizations?

options.A: Develop resources to be used in ethical decision-making processes.
options.B: Response to rapid changes in the field, investigating the ethical implications.
options.C: All of the others
options.D: Build a repository of institutional knowledge around ethical practices.

note: Ethics Board duties = ALL: develop resources + respond to changes + build knowledge repo (C). All-of-the-above.

question: Which of the following are tools of an effective media communication plan? (Select two.)

options.A: Mission statement
options.B: Video briefing
options.C: Press release
options.D: Public education campaign

note: Media plan tools: video briefing (B) + press release (C). Mission statement là internal, public ed campaign là different goal.

question: Which of the following are transport encryption protocols? (select two.)

options.A: SSH
options.B: SSH/TLS
options.C: FTP
options.D: HTTP

note: Transport encryption: SSH (A) + SSH/TLS (B). FTP và HTTP plain text, không encrypt.

question: Which approach to corporate governance is more likely to create an ethical organizational culture?

options.A: A systemic approach with incentive systems.
options.B: A board focused on legal and regulatory compliance.
options.C: An oversight committee focus on frameworks.
options.D: An oversight committee focus on rules.

note: Ethical culture cần systemic approach + incentive (A). Compliance-only board chỉ chạy theo luật, không tạo culture.

question: Which of the following is a technique used by the Python tool Alibi to produce a subset of features that will usually result in the same model prediction?

options.A: Anchor Explanations
options.B: Shapley value
options.C: Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations
options.D: Perturbation

note: Alibi tool dùng Anchor Explanations (A) — subset features cho cùng prediction. Khác SHAP (Shapley) và LIME.

question: Which subset of transparency involves describing the system to a non-expert?

options.A: Explainability
options.B: Interpretability
options.C: Auditability
options.D: Useability

note: Explainability = giải thích cho NON-EXPERT (A). Interpretability = expert hiểu, auditability = audit được, useability = dùng được.

question: Which of the following risk analysis methods use words like "likely," "unlikely," and "rare" to describe the likelihood of risk, and words like "low," "medium," and "high" to describe the impact of risk? (Select two.)

options.A: Qualitative analysis
options.B: Semi-quantitative analysis
options.C: Quantitative analysis
options.D: Semi-qualitative analysis

note: Words 'likely/unlikely/rare' và 'low/medium/high' = qualitative (a) + semi-quantitative (b). Quantitative dùng số chính xác.

question: How is employee voice different than open door policy?

options.A: Open door policy follows up with people to show how changes were implemented based on their communication.
options.B: Employee voice follows up with the people to show how changes were implemented based on their communication.

note: Employee voice (B) = follow-up cho thấy thay đổi đã thực thi từ feedback. Open door chỉ mở cửa nói, không đảm bảo thay đổi.

question: When conducting a business impact assessment (BIA), why is it important to interview or survey personnel at the ground level, not just managers and other higher-ups?

options.A: They may have more institutional knowledge about a subject than managers.
options.B: They have a greater obligation to answer questions.
options.C: They are more likely to find the time to complete an interview or survey.
options.D: They are more likely to be candid and give forthright responses.

note: BIA cần phỏng vấn ground-level vì họ có institutional knowledge nhiều hơn manager (A) — họ làm việc trực tiếp.

question: Which of the following is a key element of communications with internal and external stakeholders regarding an organization's ethics policies?

options.A: Simple to follow examples
options.B: Flexible verbiage
options.C: Dense procedural descriptions
options.D: Daily communications

note: Comm policy với stakeholder = simple-to-follow examples (A). Dense procedural / flexible verbiage / daily comm đều sai chiều.

question: Which of the following constitute internal stakeholders in the context of a code of ethics?

options.A: Designers
options.B: Regulators
options.C: Data subjects
options.D: Policymakers

note:
(internal - deisgners
external - regulators)

question: If you are doing an analysis of data to determine which of several small businesses you should purchase to gain the maximum benefit to your overall company finances, what type of analysis are you doing?

options.A: Predictive
options.B: Cumulative
options.C: Prescriptive
options.D: Descriptive

note: Prescriptive analysis = phân tích để KÊ TOA hành động (mua doanh nghiệp nào) (C). Predictive = dự đoán, descriptive = mô tả.

question: Which of the following are important aspects of resolving complex and confounding business pressures? (Select two.)

options.A: Managing expectations that not everyone can get what they want, when they want it
options.B: Prioritizing ethical and safety concerns over business desires in all cases and situations
options.C: Assuring everyone that their desires can be accommodated without compromise
options.D: Engaging with multiple stakeholders to understand their particular needs

note: Resolve business pressure: manage expectations (A) + engage multiple stakeholders (D). KHÔNG hứa 'everyone gets what they want'.

question: Which of the following describes dual-use or multipurpose data?

options.A: Data that can be easily shared with a partner or family member for mutual enjoyment.
options.B: Data that can be used in multiple devices or formats, such as a video on a Smart TV, tablet, and computer.
options.C: Data collected for one application that could also be applied to another application in a different domain.
options.D: Data that can be transformed into multiple forms, e.g. extracting audio from a video file.

note: Dual-use data = collect cho 1 app, áp dụng cho domain KHÁC (C). Vd data fitness dùng cho insurance.

question: Why do smart toys raise additional ethical concerns over those that are raised in the course of other products and services that use AI?

options.A: Children are more susceptible to manipulation and therefore need extra protective measures.
options.B: The smart toys store personal data on the device, which can be stolen.
options.C: It is difficult to obtain informed consent for the use of the smart toy.
options.D: They are used in the privacy of homes rather than in public settings, like other products or services.

note: Smart toys: trẻ em dễ bị manipulate hơn → cần extra protection (A). Đó là lý do cốt lõi, khác B/C/D.

question: Which of the following accurately describes the measures of central tendency in a normal distribution?

options.A: The mean is higher than both the median and mode.
options.B: The median is equal to the mode, and both are higher than the mean.
options.C: The mode is higher than the mean, but lower than the median.
options.D: The mean, median, and mode are the same.

note: Normal distribution: mean = median = mode (D). Đối xứng quanh trung tâm.

question: Why are deep learning and machine learning called narrow? Select two options.

options.A: Narrow can only perform the specific tasks it was designed to do.
options.B: Narrow AI can't handle situations that its training data didn't prepare it for.
options.C: Narrow AI is dependent on hand-coding of its algorithms.

note: Narrow AI vì: chỉ làm task cụ thể (A) + không xử được tình huống ngoài training (B). Hand-coding (C) không phải đặc trưng narrow.

question: Which of the following are typically true when comparing the LIME tool with the SHAP tool? (Select two.)

options.A: LIME outputs results faster than SHAP.
options.B: LIME is less accurate than SHAP.
options.C: LIME supports fewer types of models than SHAP.
options.D: LIME supports more programming languages than SHAP.

note: LIME vs SHAP: LIME nhanh hơn (A) nhưng kém chính xác hơn (B). Trade-off speed/accuracy.

question: Which of the following are cost functions used to evaluate linear regression models? (Select two.)

options.A: Accuracy
options.B: Mean squared error (MSE)
options.C: Root mean squared error (RMSE)
options.D: Recall

note: Cost function cho linear regression: MSE (B) + RMSE (C). Accuracy/recall là cho classification.

question: Why is suspicion a pitfall in communicating ethical risks to society?

options.A: Suspicion leads to use technologies ineffective.
options.B: Suspicion leads to aggressive rejection of a technology.
options.C: Suspicion involves fear of some technology that is not completely known.
options.D: Suspicion makes it impossible to convince people of the benefits of technology.

note: Suspicion = sợ vì công nghệ chưa biết (C) — root cause của resistance. A/B/D là hệ quả.

question: How does the low likelihood of a crisis relate to its potential impact?

options.A: A crisis with low likelihood tends to have high impact.
options.B: A crisis with low likelihood tends to have little to no effect on its impact.
options.C: A crisis with low likelihood tends to have low impact.
options.D: A crisis with low likelihood tends to have moderate impact.

note: Crisis low likelihood thường có HIGH impact (A) — chính vì hiếm nên khi xảy ra thì lớn (black swan).

question: What is the capability caution?

options.A: We need to take steps to ensure that humans capabilities are not limited by Al growth.
options.B: We need to remember that we don't know the full extent of what Al systems might become capable of so that we can better plan.
options.C: We need to freeze Al's capabilities at the current state so that we can ensure Al systems won't take over the world.
options.D: We need to remember that we know the full extent of what Al systems might become capable of so that we can better plan.

note: Capability caution = ta KHÔNG biết hết AI có thể làm gì → cần kế hoạch (B). Asilomar AI Principles.

question: Which of the following is a case study that best represents the principle of professional responsibility?

options.A: The Asilomar AI Principles' definition of capability caution
options.B: The American Medical Association's definition of AI as augmented intelligence
options.C: The Beijing AI Principles' tenets about the use of AI
options.D: The IEEE Ethically Aligned Design's discussion on classical ethics

note: Professional responsibility ví dụ kinh điển = AMA định nghĩa AI là 'Augmented Intelligence' (B) — đặt người ở trung tâm.

question: Which of the following are pitfalls in communicating risks to the organization? (Select two.)

options.A: Having a disjointed message.
options.B: Following up messaging with an audience.
options.C: Not paying attention to the flow of messaging.
options.D: Coordinating a message between departments.

note: Comm risk pitfall: disjointed message (A) + không chú ý flow (C). Follow-up và coordinate giữa department là TỐT.

question: Which of the following are potential training data inadequacies that you should communicate to your users in support of transparency? (Select two.)

options.A: The amount of time it took to collect the data.
options.B: Any missing values in the dataset and how they were handled.
options.C: The relatively large size of a dataset used in training.
options.D: Any known bias in the sample data.

note: Training data inadequacy phải nói: missing values + cách handle (B) + known bias (D). Time/size không phải bias risk.

question: Which of the following is a benefit of using focus groups over surveys?

options.A: They allow for deeper engagement with the target demographic community.
options.B: They are a more scalable option for the collection of information.
options.C: They are an easier way to collect information from a target audience.
options.D: They can provide more data through structured information gathering.

note: Focus group > survey vì deeper engagement với community (A). Survey scale tốt hơn nhưng nông.

question: In using AI-enabled solutions within the context of medical imaging analysis, which of the following is the most important ethical consideration?

options.A: Explainability
options.B: Privacy
options.C: Security
options.D: Bias

note: Medical imaging AI: ưu tiên Explainability (A) — bác sĩ phải hiểu vì sao model nói thế trước khi điều trị.

question: If you were to place the task "Apply security patch to system" on an attack tree diagram, what category would it be assigned to?

options.A: Threat
options.B: Vector
options.C: Control
options.D: Controller

note: Apply security patch = Control (C) trên attack tree. Threat = mối đe dọa, vector = đường tấn công, controller = người điều khiển.

question: Which of the following best describes capability caution as referenced in the Asilomar AI Principles?

options.A: Should there be a greater reliance on AI, measures should be taken to ensure that humans are still capable of finding work.
options.B: We should keep limits on on what artificial general intelligence (AGI) is capable of.
options.C: If there is no understanding of the internal mechanisms of AI, then AI development should be halted.
options.D: Given a lack of consensus, we should avoid strong assumptions regarding upper limits on future AI capabilities.

note: Asilomar capability caution: KHÔNG strong assumption về upper limit AI (D) — vì chưa có consensus.

question: What make ethics training effective?

options.A: Keep the content simple and direct.
options.B: Keep sessions interactive.
options.C: Keep sessions short and focused.
options.D: All

note: Ethics training hiệu quả = ALL: simple + interactive + short-focused (D). Combo cả ba.

question: What is the primary advantage of radioactive data tracing over past techniques that modify input in order to determine whether or not that input was used in training?

options.A: Radioactive data tracing doesn't impact a model's performance.
options.B: Radioactive data tracing can be used to modify the label.
options.C: Radioactive data tracing targets language-based input.
options.D: Radioactive data tracing makes the modification perceptible to human beings.

note: Radioactive data tracing không impact performance (A) — đó là ưu thế chính so với techniques cũ làm lệch input.

question: Which of the following principle sets are important for the creation of open data that is widely utilized?

options.B: FATE
options.D: FAIR

note: Open data principle = FAIR (D). ISO 27001/27017 là security, FATE là Fairness/Accountability/Transparency/Ethics framework khác.

question: Which of the following describes an illusory-correlation bias?

options.A: When you incorrectly assume a correlation because there is an illusory confounding variable.
options.B: When you correlate variables that do not exist in your data set.
options.C: When you correlate a variable with a confounding variable.
options.D: When you incorrectly assume a cause and effect relationship because two variables are correlated.

note: Illusory correlation = giả định cause-effect vì 2 biến correlate (D). 'Correlation ≠ causation' bias.

question: An attacker fools an AI-based anti-spam system into thinking the attacker's email messages are benign. This enables the attacker's emails to reach their destination(s) without being blocked. What type of adversarial machine learning is this?

options.A: Evasion
options.B: Poisoning
options.C: Spoofing
options.D: Reasoning

note: Spam evasion = attacker fools system thinking benign (A). Poisoning = đầu độc training data, evasion = đánh ở inference time.

question: Which of the following types of malware monitors a user's behavior without their knowledge or permission?

options.A: Virus
options.B: Spyware
options.C: Ransomware
options.D: Trojan horse

note: Spyware (B) = monitor user behavior bí mật. Virus = lây lan, ransomware = đòi tiền chuộc, trojan = giả dạng.

question: Which of the following metrics is used to evaluate a linear regression machine learning model?

options.A: Receiver operating characteristic (ROC)
options.B: Cost function
options.C: Goodhart's Law
options.D: Accuracy

note: Linear regression dùng cost function (B) — RMSE/MSE. ROC/accuracy là classification.

question: Which of the following resources does the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) provide to organizations?

options.A: Industrial configurations
options.B: Measurement technologies
options.C: Reference materials
options.D: Security tools

note: NIST cung cấp Reference materials (C) — chuẩn đo lường. Không bán security tools hay configs.

question: In Al, the principle of privacy is most commonly referred to in the context of which of the following concepts?

options.A: Data protection
options.B: Transparency
options.C: Human control
options.D: Personal protection

note: Privacy in AI thường gắn với Data Protection (A) — bảo vệ data cá nhân.

question: Which of the following constitute external stakeholders in the context of a code of ethics?

options.A: Regulators
options.B: Developers
options.C: Designers
options.D: Business managers

note:
(internal - deisgners
external - regulators)

question: In the following scatter plot, the Grossincome variable is plotted against the Revenue variable. What type of correlation does this plot suggest?

options.A: There is a weak positive correlation between both variables
options.B: There is a strong positive correlation between both variables
options.C: There is a strong negative correlation between both variables.
options.D: There is a weak negative correlation between both variables

note: Scatter plot positive correlation mạnh = B (strong positive). Cần nhìn slope rõ và scatter chặt.

question: Which of the following is a platform that includes hundreds of security exploit tools?

options.A: Wireshark
options.B: Nmap
options.C: Zenmap
options.D: Kal Linux

note: Kali Linux (D) = distro với hundreds of security tools. Wireshark/Nmap/Zenmap là single tools.

question: Which of the following situations would most likely require communication to external stakeholders? (Select two.)

options.A: The organization's change management strategy has been modified.
options.B: The organization plans to adopt a new ethical framework to support the implementation of an AI system.
options.C: The organization's data-driven service will receive an update that enhances its accuracy.
options.D: The organization's AI product has a defect and needs to be recalled.

note: External stakeholder communicate khi: enhance accuracy update (C) + recall defect (D). Internal change management không cần báo external.

question: Which team typically oversees penetration test operations and adherence to the rules of engagement (ROE)?

options.A: Blue team
options.B: White team
options.C: Purple team
options.D: Red team

note: White team (B) oversee pentest và ROE adherence. Red = attacker, Blue = defender, Purple = collaboration.

question: Which of the following is an ethical benefit of incorporating fair competition considerations in a data-driven technology ecosystem?

options.A: Lowering product and service costs for the users
options.B: Accelerating investments in research and development
options.C: Encouraging product or service differentiation
options.D: Consolidating products and services under a single organization

note: Fair competition ethical benefit = encourage product/service differentiation (C) — đa dạng lựa chọn.

question: An ethical impact assessment is _____

options.A: Focused on preventing future problems.
options.B: Focused on the impact of past ethical risks.
options.C: Focused on identifying the causes of past errors.

note: Ethical impact assessment = focus PREVENT future problems (A). Khác post-mortem nhìn lại.

question: Which of the following describe corporate hegemony? (Select two.)

options.A: Consolidating interests through mergers and acquisitions
options.B: Locking out smaller players, leading to monopolies or cartels
options.C: Spending large sums on corporate branding and marketing
options.D: Making multiple investments in a similar space to improve the outcomes of success

note: Corporate hegemony: M&A consolidation (A) + monopoly/cartel (B). Marketing spend và investment đa dạng không phải hegemony.

question: What does it mean to say that human rights are inalienable? (Select two.)

options.A: Inalienable rights are inherent in all human beings.
options.B: Inalienable rights cannot be taken away except in extreme circumstances.
options.C: Inalienable rights are conditional.
options.D: Inalienable rights are derived from tradition.

note: Inalienable rights: inherent in all humans (A) + không thể tước trừ extreme circumstances (B). Không conditional, không từ tradition.

question: What is theTuring test used for?

options.A: To test whether an Al system is availobility.
options.B: To test whether an Al system is trustworthy.
options.C: To test whether an Al system is capoble of tricking a person into thinking it's another person.
options.D: To test whether an Al system is capable of thinking like a human being

note: Turing test = thinking like a human (D). Dù option C nói 'tricking person', đáp án chính thống là 'thinking like human'.

question: How do AI and other data-driven technologies use probability?

options.A: By determining the objective likelihood of some event happening
options.B: By providing a model of belief about the likelihood of some event happening
options.C: By guaranteeing that some event will occur with 100% likelihood
options.D: By estimating the likelihood of some event happening without input data

note: AI dùng probability = model of belief về likelihood (B). Không guarantee 100%, không objective likelihood.

question: Which of the following are consequences of saying that someone or something has moral agency? (Select two.)

options.A: The moral agent is capable of determining right and wrong.
options.B: The moral agent can be held responsible for their actions.
options.C: The moral agent acts in a morally correct manner.
options.D: The moral agent follows a deontological code of ethics.

note: Moral agency hệ quả: xác định right/wrong (A) + chịu trách nhiệm hành động (B). KHÔNG yêu cầu acts morally correct.

question: What is ambient intelligence?

options.A: Linked IOT technology that conducts covert surveillance for the govermment on the people within its environment.
options.B: Linked IOT technology that always on and acting to personalize the experience for the people within its environment
options.C: Linked IOT technology that collects and stores data about a home or office environment.
options.D: Linked IOT technology that collects and stores data about campus environment.

note: Ambient intelligence = IoT always-on personalize trải nghiệm (B). Khác surveillance đơn thuần.

question: Which of the following describe important aspects in the role of an ethical AI engineer? (Select two.)

options.A: Building and maintaining computational hardware.
options.B: Writing new equations to express intelligence.
options.C: Keeping up with the latest developments and vulnerabilities.
options.D: Cleaning and sorting data, and auditing for bias.

note: Ethical AI engineer: keep up developments/vulnerabilities (C) + clean/sort/audit data (D). Không xây hardware hay 'write equations'.

question: Which of the following describe important aspects of why emerging technologies are so capable and powerful? (Select two.)

options.A: They can automate very complex operations.
options.B: They may be able to self-improve by learning from data.
options.C: They are exciting and captivating to many people.
options.D: They can displace workers by performing their jobs more efficiently.

note: Emerging tech mạnh vì: automate complex ops (A) + self-improve from data (B). 'Exciting' và 'displace workers' là hệ quả, không phải 'why powerful'.

question: Which of the following are desired qualities when choosing a spokesperson for your organization? (Select two.)

options.A: Defensive when challenged with difficult situations
options.B: Patient when addressing tough questions or concerns
options.C: Lighthearted when dealing with serious topics
options.D: Articulate when communicating important details

note: Spokesperson tốt: patient (B) + articulate (D). Defensive/lighthearted về topic serious đều sai.

question: In which of the following could STRIDE or VAST classifications be applied?

options.A: Network optimization tools
options.B: Black box mitigation methods
options.C: Bias mitigation techniques
options.D: Threat modeling or analysis tools

note: STRIDE/VAST = threat modeling tools (D). KHÔNG phải bias mitigation hay network optimization.

question: Management asks someone to do a data-related task. Which of the following would likely be ethically problematic? (Select two.)

options.A: Aggregate data together.
options.B: Change data to another format.
options.C: Manipulate data or alter its interpretation.
options.D: Delete any erroneous data.

note: Ethically problematic: manipulate/alter interpretation (C) + delete erroneous data (D — vì che giấu evidence). Aggregate/format change là OK.

question: Which of the following describes ethics washing?

options.A: No attempt is made towards genuine ethical action.
options.B: Superficial promises are replaced by genuine ethical action.
options.C: Genuine ethical action is used to wash over previous mistakes.
options.D: Genuine ethical action gets replaced by superficial promises.

note: Ethics washing = genuine action bị thay bằng superficial promises (D). 'No attempt' là ethics avoidance, không phải washing.

question: If you were faced with an AI system that identifies exactly what individuals will be infected in a pandemic of a novel disease before the first case occurs, what kind of Al is this?

options.A: Narrow Al
options.B: General Al
options.C: Basic Al
options.D: Superintelligence

note: Identify case BEFORE first occurs = Superintelligence (D) — vượt mọi khả năng human. Narrow chỉ làm task hẹp đã biết.

question: How can the human resources department help build an ethical organizational culture?

options.A: Hiring candidates based on their technical skills.
options.B: Hiring candidates based on their soft skills.
options.C: Hiring candidates based on their culture fit.
options.D: Hiring candidates based on their leadership skills.

note: HR build culture qua hire dựa trên CULTURE FIT (C). Không chỉ technical/soft/leadership.

question: You plan on streamlining your company's product experience, but you also want to uphold the agency and autonomy of your users. Which of the following actions would uphold these principles? (Select two.)

options.A: Enabling government agencies to have a personalized interface with software
options.B: Refraining from guiding users into something they didn't wish for or intend
options.C: Respecting the right of the user to choose and customize their experiences
options.D: Applying machine intelligence to simulate customer behavior

note: Streamline + tôn trọng autonomy: không hướng user vào điều họ không muốn (B) + tôn trọng quyền customize (C). Bảo vệ user agency.

question: You have collected social media posts with a specific hashtag for an analysis of the words used in the post. What kind of data is that?

options.A: Unstructured
options.B: Structured
options.C: Semi-Structured
options.D: Data Structured

note: Social media post hashtag = Unstructured data (A) — text tự do. Structured = bảng, semi = JSON.

question: Which of the following, by itself, qualifies as personally identifiable information (PII)?

options.A: Temperature readings for an office building
options.B: A user's customer ID in an online ordering system
options.C: System events added to a log
options.D: A user's home address

note: PII tự thân: home address (D). Customer ID / log event / temperature không identify trực tiếp một người.

question: What percentage of frameworks include transparency?
note:
(transparency: 85%
core: 50%)

question: What percentage of frameworks include privacy as core tenet?
note:
(transparency: 85%
core: 50%)

question: Which of the following measures is best suited for reporting results to an audience?

options.A: Mode
options.B: Variance
options.C: Standard deviation
options.D: Median

note: Report to audience = Standard deviation (C) — đơn vị giống data, dễ hiểu. Variance bị bình phương.

question: Which of the following are examples of how AI can limit human autonomy? (Select two.)

options.A: Weapon systems can limit human autonomy as humans may not have decision-making capability or understand the decision making.
options.B: AI systems might impact certain vulnerable groups such as the elderly and children differently than the rest of the population, which could limit those groups' autonomy.
options.C: AI systems can assist individuals with automated, repetitive, or dangerous tasks.
options.D: AI systems can perform tasks that humans cannot, such as processing millions of data records in a matter of seconds.

note: AI giới hạn autonomy: weapon system tước decision-making (A) + impact vulnerable group khác biệt (B). C/D là benefit, không phải limit autonomy.

question: When it comes to protecting private data, which of the following could be the root cause of a problem?
